etymology.description: semantic 艹(艹) (cǎo, "grass") + phonetic 亡(亡) (wáng) -> edge of a knife
etymology.interpretation: The character 芒(máng, "edge of a knife") uses the 艹(艹) (cǎo, "grass") component to highlight its relation to plant life or natural elements, while 亡(亡) (wáng) provides the phonetic indication. Originally it may have depicted something like 方 but evolved to illustrate the thinness or sharpness of grass edges, metaphorically linking to the sharp edge of a knife.

examples.chinese: 刀的芒在阳光下闪闪发光(刀的芒在陽光下閃閃發光)。
examples.pinyin: Dāo de máng zài yángguāng xià shǎnshǎn fāguāng.
examples.english: The edge of the knife glistens in the sunlight.

examples.chinese: 我们要小心刀的芒(我們要小心刀的芒)，因为它非常锋利(因為它非常鋒利)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yào xiǎoxīn dāo de máng, yīnwèi tā fēicháng fēnglì.
examples.english: We must be careful of the knife's edge because it is very sharp.

examples.chinese: 他用手指轻轻触摸刀的芒(他用手指輕輕觸摸刀的芒)，感受到它的锋利(感受到它的鋒利)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng shǒuzhǐ qīngqīng chùmō dāo de máng, gǎnshòudào tā de fēnglì.
examples.english: He gently touched the blade's edge with his finger and felt its sharpness.
